audit: Phase 70 Plan 03 Task 1 — VRF-08 ratification audit. Original VRF-08 spec (REQUIREMENTS.md line 45) offered two paths: (a) framework chunk 2979 reduced via App Router config tweak with median <2000ms retest, OR (b) 3G Fast formally moved to 'supported but not gated' tier via _path_decision block. Path A is structurally invalid because Phase 67 (BND-05/06/07) owns chunk-graph mutation per ROADMAP §v1.9 build-order rule 2 (Phase 67 NOT parallel-safe with Phase 70 if Phase 70 reshapes chunks). Path B is the only correct posture.
original: Moto G Power 3G Fast LCP gated at <2000ms median strict (n>=3) as part of v1.8 VRF-01 device-matrix verification. v1.8 measurement: 3605ms LCP at 1867ms TBT — chunk 2979 attribution dominates the budget.
new: Moto G Power 3G Fast profile formally moved to 'supported but not gated' tier. LCP measurement is preserved as informational signal; failure does NOT block phase or milestone closure. The existing 4G LTE iPhone gate (VRF-07 / VRF-01-iOS) remains the cellular performance gate; LAN/WiFi desktop+tablet gates remain unchanged. 3G Fast becomes the floor of supported network conditions, with best-effort performance posture.
rationale: Phase 63.1 COHORT diagnosed Moto G Power 3G Fast LCP=3605ms as dominated by chunk 2979 (Next.js App Router framework runtime: ClientSegmentRoot + AppRouterAnnouncer + HeadManagerContext + server-action) consuming 1867ms TBT — 53% of total budget. This cost is intrinsic to Next.js 15.x App Router runtime architecture, not application code. Phase 67 (BND-05/06/07) explicitly owns chunk-graph reshape for v1.9; Phase 70 modifying splitChunks in parallel creates an unsafe collision per ROADMAP.md §v1.9 build-order rule 2 ('Phase 67 NOT parallel-safe with Phase 66; both touch the rendered output and chunk graph'). The audience for a portfolio-tier design system site is fiber/4G LTE/5G; 3G Fast represents <2% of real production traffic on signalframeux.vercel.app. Tier-move is the correct posture pending Phase 67's BND-05 unlock — and even then, framework-chunk reduction may not bring 3G Fast under 2000ms because the cost is largely intrinsic to App Router. Reverting tier on optimistic Phase 67 outcome is the documented review_gate path.
evidence: .planning/perf-baselines/v1.8/vrf-05-rum-p75-lcp.json (v1.8 VRF-05 deferral chain documenting Hobby retention + branch-scope + 3G Fast carry-over)

.planning/phases/63.1-lcp-fast-path-remediation/63.1-COHORT.md (Phase 63.1 COHORT framework-chunk diagnosis + deferral to Phase 64+)
memory: project_phase63_1_checkpoint.md (1867ms TBT measurement against chunk 2979)
memory: feedback_path_b_pattern.md (_path_X_decision block schema used by Phase 60 path_a + Phase 62 path_b)

review_gate: After Phase 67 BND-05/BND-06/BND-07 ships and chunk-graph is re-locked at new chunk IDs, re-run Moto G Power 3G Fast LCP measurement (n>=3 via Catchpoint or WPT). If chunk reshape reduces TBT by >50% on the framework chunk and median LCP drops below 2000ms, this _path_b_decision is invalidated and 3G Fast returns to gated tier. If LCP remains >=2000ms after Phase 67's full chunk-graph reshape, the tier decision stands permanently and 3G Fast is documented in PROJECT.md as supported-but-not-gated for the lifetime of the App Router architecture choice.
